introPage.title: JOMAP
introPage.subtitle: A community based locations and activities
introPage.description: A unified digital community platform dedicated and tailored to the Jordanian context. The platform integrates interactive geographical services with activities and events management, enhanced by a collaborative social environment that hosts two main types of entities: profit-seeking commercial businesses that benefit from an innovative promotional system offering exclusive and attractive discounts, and non-profit volunteer teams and organizations that organize community initiatives and events. This actively contributes to stimulating the local economy, supporting youth and volunteer work, and creates a seamless link connecting location discovery, activity participation, and exclusive offers.
introPage.simpleDescription:
JO MAP is a platform that brings all locations, events, and activities happening in Jordan into one place.

Many exciting activities, offers, restaurants, or unique spots in the country struggle to reach people. At the same time, people want to go out and have fun but don't know where to go, simply because there is no real platform connecting them together.

Here comes the idea of JO MAP: the app immediately connects you to your current area, focusing on all nearby offers, events, and locations.

We didn't build maps from scratch or waste time reinventing the wheel; we integrated Google Maps, leveraged its powerful data, and built this interactive system on top. This is the true role of software engineering: combining existing solutions to deliver a real, useful product that serves the user, the business owner, and strengthens the Jordanian community.
introPage.rolesTitle: The Three Main Roles in JOMAP Ecosystem

user.title: The Explorer / Regular User
user.subtitle: Mobile App User
user.desc: Represents the wide audience of youth, families, and tourists interested in exploring Jordan and community interaction.
user.actions: Explores interactive maps, joins events, redeems local offers, posts daily stories, and uses Nashmi AI to plan trips.

owner.title: Business Owner / Entity
owner.subtitle: Mobile Business Panel
owner.desc: Represents local businesses (restaurants, cafes, shops) and non-profit youth/volunteer event organizers.
owner.actions: Registers business on maps, launches geolocated promotional offers, manages events, and tracks performance on mobile.

admin.title: Platform Administrator (Admin)
admin.subtitle: Web Control Dashboard
admin.desc: Represents the regulatory and monitoring entity ensuring safety, reliability, and quality of platform content.
admin.actions: Moderates the ecosystem via Web Admin Dashboard, approves locations, reviews reports, and monitors global metrics.

introPage.pillarsTitle: The Four Pillars of the Project

community.title: Social Network
community.desc: Sharing moments and experiences to build genuine interaction

maps.title: Interactive Locations
maps.desc: Smart geographical exploration of landmarks and services in Jordan

events.title: Events & Activities
events.desc: Organizing and participating in local and youth initiatives

offers.title: Offers & Discounts
offers.desc: A smart promotional system targeting users geographically

p1.title: Absence of Unified Platforms
p1.desc: Lack of a single application that integrates activities, events, exclusive offers, and interactive maps in one place.

p2.title: Geographic Access Difficulty
p2.desc: Difficult for local business owners and projects to reach their target audience geographically.

p3.title: Lack of Community Interaction
p3.desc: Absence of an active social and participatory environment that connects individuals with their local community.

p4.title: Content Credibility Challenges
p4.desc: Difficult to moderate content and verify offers and locations without a unified authority.

p5.title: Challenges in Volunteer Work
p5.desc: Difficulty organizing and promoting youth initiatives and volunteer work due to the lack of a centralized platform connecting non-profit teams with local volunteers.

p6.title: Limited Modern Tech & AI Integration
p6.desc: Limited integration of modern technologies (like real-time geofencing and AI recommendation engines) to smart-guide users to personalized experiences.

o1.title: Unified Integrated Hub
o1.desc: Integrating maps, events, and offers with a community network in a single application.

o2.title: Boost Tourism & Social Work
o2.desc: Promoting local tourism, family activities, and supporting youth volunteer initiatives.

o3.title: Support Local Economy
o3.desc: Enabling merchants to promote their offers with smart geolocated targeting to attract customers.

o4.title: Ensure Quality & Trust
o4.desc: Providing an admin moderation dashboard to verify the credibility of locations and offers.

o5.title: Activate Youth & Volunteer Work
o5.desc: Enabling and promoting the culture of volunteerism and youth initiatives by providing tools that make it easy for community teams to organize and promote their activities directly to interested local audiences.

o6.title: Strengthening Jordanian Community Bonds
o6.desc: Creating a digital link that supports local initiatives and connects members of the Jordanian community, fostering social cohesion and positive interaction across all governorates.

buildPage.title: Details of Build
buildPage.subtitle: Built from scratch following Software Engineering principles and the full SDLC across two academic graduation projects
buildPage.intro: JO MAP was engineered from the ground up following rigorous Software Engineering principles and the global Systems Development Life Cycle (SDLC) phases. The entire project — from ideation to testing — was documented, versioned, and managed using Jira, Git, and GitHub across Graduation Project 1 (Analysis & Architecture) and Graduation Project 2 (Implementation & Testing) at The Hashemite University.

dev.name: Development Machines
dev.desc: Laptop or Desktop Computer (8 GB RAM or higher)

net.name: Internet Connection
net.desc: Stable internet connection for development, testing, and API usage

backend.title: Backend Services
backend.desc: Spring Boot, Firebase Authentication, Firestore Database

phase1.title: Introduction & Project Motivation
phase1.text: Initiated the project by defining the core vision of JO MAP as a localized community and activity platform. Analyzed problem statement regarding fragmentation of mapping services and event listings in Jordan, establishing the project's aims, objectives, and scope.

phase2.title: Literature & Existing Systems Review
phase2.text: Evaluated existing applications (OsmAnd, Balady, Eventbrite, LinkedIn) to identify common limitations such as fragmentation of mapping and activity services, lack of community integration, and limited localized personalization.

phase3.title: Requirement Engineering & Analysis
phase3.text: Elicited functional and non-functional requirements (usability, performance, security, reliability, availability). Documented 15 detailed use case specifications (Tables 6-20) representing user, business owner, and admin roles.

phase4.title: System Architecture & UML Modeling
phase4.text: Designed software architecture logical view (MVVM component diagram), process view (communication diagrams), and physical view (deployment diagram). Developed class diagrams, entity-relationship database diagrams, and state transition lifecycle diagrams.

phase5.title: Implementation Plan & Technology Stack
phase5.text: Defined major implementation tasks and resources. Selected Kotlin & Jetpack Compose for frontend development, Spring Boot and Firebase (Authentication, Firestore Database, and Storage) for backend services, and Google Maps API for map integration.

phase6.title: Testing Plan & Quality Assurance
phase6.text: Formulated a structured testing plan covering black-box techniques (Equivalence Partitioning, Boundary Value Analysis, Use Case Testing) and white-box methods. Defined automation strategies using Espresso, Appium, JUnit 5, Mockito, and Postman.

phase7.title: Conclusion & Future Work
phase7.text: Summarized project outcomes and established roadmap for mobile app execution. Outlined long-term enhancements such as multi-language support (Arabic & English), advanced AI recommendations, offline capabilities, and iOS/web versions.

umlPage.title: UML & Architecture Diagrams
umlPage.subtitle: Technical system blueprints mapping stakeholders, interactions, components, and data structures of JO MAP

stakeholder.title: Stakeholder Onion Diagram
stakeholder.explain: The Stakeholder Onion Diagram organizes the project participants into concentric layers of proximity to the core system. The innermost layer contains the primary actors directly interacting with JO MAP (Users, Business Owners, Admins). The middle layer represents project facilitators (University Supervisor, Development Team). The outer layer lists external technical stakeholders and integrated services (Google Maps API, Firebase, ChatGPT API, Payment Gateway, Notification Service).

usecase.title: System Use Case Diagram
usecase.explain: The Use Case Diagram defines the functional scope of JO MAP and outlines how different actors interact with system boundaries. It details 15 major use case specifications, highlighting critical operations such as Location/Activity Management for Business Owners, Interactive Map browsing and feedback submission for Users, and account/content moderation for Administrators.

component.title: Component Diagram (Logical View)
component.explain: Following the MVVM architectural pattern, the Component Diagram illustrates the separation of concerns across different system layers. It details the interaction between Views, ViewModels, Repositories, Models, and external API Services, showing how data flow is decoupled to ensure maintainability, testability, and system extensibility.

process.title: Process View (Activity Diagram)
process.explain: The Process View Diagram illustrates the dynamic flow of operations and runtime execution paths within JO MAP across View, ViewModel, Repository, and Model layers. It models key system workflows including: (1) Location creation, admin review, and notification dispatch, (2) User registration and check-existence validation, (3) Authentication and login controllers, and (4) Activity registration and payment transaction confirmation loops.

deployment.title: Deployment Diagram (Physical View)
deployment.explain: The Deployment Diagram depicts the physical distribution of hardware nodes, client interfaces, backend servers, databases, and third-party integrations. It illustrates how the Android Mobile App client communicates with the Spring Boot Backend Server Cluster via HTTPS REST APIs, and how data is persisted across Firestore and Cloud Storage, alongside external connection protocols.

class.title: Domain Class Diagram
class.explain: The UML Class Diagram models the object-oriented structure of the JO MAP system. It defines the attributes, methods, relationships, and multiplicities between core domain classes including User, Business Owner, Admin, Post, Location, Activity, and Governorate, establishing a robust blueprint for data modeling.

er.title: Database Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD)
er.explain: The Entity-Relationship (ER) Diagram defines the relational database schema, mapping the logical entities and relationships that store platform data. It details primary and foreign keys, attributes, and cardinalities between core tables such as Users, Locations, Activities, Comments, Community Posts, Chats, and Notifications.

seq_search_location.title: Sequence: Search & View Location
seq_search_location.explain: Illustrates the interaction steps for searching and viewing locations. (1) The user inputs a keyword, which View forwards to ViewModel. (2) ViewModel queries Repository, which retrieves matching records from the SpecialDataJoMap Model. (3) Results return up to be displayed. (4) The user selects a specific location, triggering an additional request to query detailed page information from the pageInformation Model.

seq_create_location.title: Sequence: Create Location Listing
seq_create_location.explain: Models the process for adding new location listings. (1) User enters data via the View, which ViewModel validates and sends to Repository. (2) Repository stores the location with a 'pending' state. (3) Admin Service reviews the listing, leading to an alternative path: [Approved] publishes the location in the Model and sends a confirmation email via Notification Service, or [Rejected] registers the rejection state and notifies the owner.

seq_submit_comment.title: Sequence: Submit Comment & Report
seq_submit_comment.explain: Defines the comment submission and moderation workflow. (1) Comment details are sent from View to ViewModel, validated, and saved in the ReviewModel via Repository. (2) If a user reports a comment, ViewModel opens a review ticket in Admin Service. (3) Admin reviews the ticket, leading to an alternative path: [Approved] deletes the inappropriate comment and notifies the user, or [Rejected] keeps the comment and notifies the reporter.

seq_post_interaction.title: Sequence: Post Creation & Interaction
seq_post_interaction.explain: Illustrates social interactions on the community feed. (1) User creates a post which is validated by ViewModel, saved to the CommunityPost Model via Repository, and published to the feed. (2) When another user interacts with the post (e.g., likes, comments), a new interaction request is processed by ViewModel and Repository, updating the interaction counter and refreshing the view.

seq_register_activity.title: Sequence: Registration for Activity
seq_register_activity.explain: Tracks the step-by-step activity registration process. (1) User requests registration; ViewModel verifies availability and capacity in the Activity Model via Repository. (2) If capacity is available, ViewModel prompts for payment. (3) Payment Service processes the transaction, and upon success, Repository adds the registration record, confirming the booking and triggering an email confirmation via Notification Service.

state_location_lifecycle.title: State Lifecycle: Location Listing
state_location_lifecycle.explain: Traces the states of a location listing. (1) It begins in 'Draft' state where edit/fix operations occur. (2) Once submitted, it enters 'PendingApproval' awaiting admin action. (3) Admin can 'Reject' it (reverting it to Draft for edits) or 'Approve' it. (4) Approved listings are 'Published' and visible. (5) Published locations can be 'Deactivated' (and reactivated later) or 'Deleted' permanently.

state_comment_lifecycle.title: State Lifecycle: Comment
state_comment_lifecycle.explain: Defines comment moderation states. (1) A comment starts in 'Created' and goes through 'AutomatedCheck'. (2) It is auto-approved into 'Approved' and then 'Published', or 'Rejected' (reverting to Created for edits). (3) Once published, comments can be edited (entering 'Edited' state), reported (entering 'Reported' then 'UnderReview' state), or deleted.

state_post_lifecycle.title: State Lifecycle: Post Moderation
state_post_lifecycle.explain: Traces community post moderation states. (1) Starts in 'Created' and moves to 'Published' upon submission. (2) If flagged, it enters 'Reported' state. (3) Admins review reported posts, moving them to 'UnderReview'. (4) The post is either dismissed (reverting back to Published) or 'Removed' permanently from the community feed.

state_activity_lifecycle.title: State Lifecycle: Activity
state_activity_lifecycle.explain: Traces the lifecycle of a community activity/event. (1) Starts in 'Draft' and moves to 'PendingApproval' upon submission. (2) Upon admin approval, it enters 'Approved' and then 'Published'. (3) Published activities can be completed ('Completed'), cancelled ('Cancelled'), or postponed ('Postponed') before rescheduled.

state_registration_lifecycle.title: State Lifecycle: Activity Registration
state_registration_lifecycle.explain: Illustrates the states of user registration for activities. (1) Registration is initiated ('RegistrationInitiated'). (2) If free, it bypasses payment and enters 'Registered'. (3) If paid, it enters 'PendingPayment' until payment succeeds ('Registered') or is cancelled ('Cancelled'). (4) Once completed, the status is set to 'Completed'.

interactiveMap.title: Interactive Map of Jordan

reviews.title: Reviews & Ratings

ownerDashboard.title: Owner Dashboard
ownerDashboard.text: Owners can add locations, upload photos, and update their business information.

systemAdmin.title: System Administration
systemAdmin.text: Administrators approve locations, manage users, and monitor reports.

directComm.title: Direct Communication
directComm.text: Users can communicate with business owners and ask questions directly.

roadmapPage.title: JOMAP App Flow for Explorers
roadmapPage.subtitle: This map shows how users navigate within JOMAP from opening the app to geographical exploration, saving favorites, and contacting business owners.

openApp.title: Open JOMAP App
openApp.text: The user opens the mobile app and begins the exploration journey.

login.title: Login / Create Account
login.text: The user creates an account or logs in to access personalized features.

explore.title: Explore Jordan's Map
explore.text: The user browses Jordan's governorates using the interactive map.

search.title: Search for Places
search.text: The user can search for tourist sites, restaurants, hotels, or markets.

selectLocation.title: Select Location
selectLocation.text: The user taps on a location marker or card to open place details.

viewDetails.title: View Details
viewDetails.text: The app displays photos, descriptions, active offers, and community events.

saveRate.title: Save to Favorites
saveRate.text: The user saves their favorite places and activities for future reference.

contactOwner.title: Direct Communication
contactOwner.text: The user can contact the place owner or event organizer directly for inquiries.

complete.title: Experience Complete
complete.text: The user finishes their journey with an easier and smarter way to discover Jordan.

ownerFlowPage.title: Business Dashboard Flow
ownerFlowPage.subtitle: This flow shows the steps business owners take from account registration and document verification, to managing locations and publishing promotional offers and events.

registerBusiness.title: Submit Business Registration
registerBusiness.text: The owner starts by submitting a complete registration request: business name, category, precise location on the map, governorate, working hours, logo, cover photo, and social media links.

login.title: Login to Personal Account
login.text: After admin approval, the owner logs in with their personal account to securely access the dashboard and manage their listed business.

dashboard.title: Business Control Dashboard
dashboard.text: Access the personal dashboard which provides a comprehensive overview and real-time analytics of business performance, including total offer views, total clicks, and quick shortcuts to manage content, ratings, and data.

manageLocation.title: Manage & Update Location
manageLocation.text: Continuously manage and update location details to keep information accurate for visitors: edit contact info, update working hours, change promotional images and cover photo to keep the profile fresh.

addEvent.title: Organize Activities & Events
addEvent.text: The owner organizes activities and events which are submitted for Admin review. If approved they are published immediately; if rejected they are locked and the Owner must create a new one from scratch.

manageSubscribers.title: Manage Event Subscribers
manageSubscribers.text: A dedicated panel for each event allowing the organizer to manage attendance requests and registered participants individually or in bulk.

createOffer.title: Launch Geo-targeted Offers
createOffer.text: Create exclusive promotional offers to attract customers in the business's geographic area. Control discount percentages, renew expired offers with one click, and track their performance.

notifications.title: Receive Instant Notifications
notifications.text: Receive real-time notifications to stay updated on everything around the business: approval/rejection of registration or pending edits, new event registrations, and new ratings or comments from visitors.

viewReviews.title: Monitor Visitor Reviews
viewReviews.text: Analyze star ratings and written reviews from visitors, track rating edit history to see how customer impressions changed after resolving issues, and respond to build strong interactive relationships.

complete.title: Experience Complete & Success
complete.text: The business owner achieves maximum promotional and investment benefit through ongoing interaction of app visitors with their offers and events, leading to greater geographic reach and business growth.

adminFlowPage.title: Moderation & System Management
adminFlowPage.subtitle: This flow illustrates how system administrators review business credentials, moderate user-generated content, and monitor analytics to keep the platform safe.

login.title: Admin Dashboard Login
login.text: Securely log into the web administration panel.

analytics.title: Live Analytics & Metrics
analytics.text: Monitor platform traffic, active users, locations, and real-time dashboard analytics.

manageLocations.title: Verify & Approve Locations
manageLocations.text: Audit registration requests: Approve to let Owners publish live, or Reject with feedback.

manageEvents.title: Review Activities & Events
manageEvents.text: Verify event guidelines. Approves to publish, or Rejects. If rejected, Owner must create a new one.

manageUsers.title: User Moderation
manageUsers.text: View user profiles, active roles, and suspend/unblock accounts to ensure community safety.

moderation.title: Reports & General Moderation
moderation.text: Resolve user reports, delete flag messages, and keep the feed clean of inappropriate content.

complete.title: Admin Operations Complete
complete.text: Successfully secure the ecosystem, maintain data verification, and drive business/user growth.

screensPage.title: Application Screens
screensPage.subtitle: These screens show the main user experience within JoMap, from opening the app to exploring places and managing the user account.

home.title: Home Screen
home.text: The home screen that welcomes users and provides quick access to key features.

map.title: Interactive Map
map.text: Users can explore Jordan using an interactive map with location markers.

details.title: Location Details
details.text: Displays photos, description, ratings, reviews, and communication options for each place.

profile.title: Profile Screen
profile.text: Users can manage profile data, favorites, reviews, and personal settings.

solutionsPage.title: Technical Solutions
solutionsPage.subtitle: Complex Problems We Faced and How We Solved Them

notifications.problem: Problem: Notification Congestion Between User Types
notifications.problemText: How can we deliver social notifications to regular users and business notifications (ratings, offers) to owners without overlap or disturbance?
notifications.solutionText: Building completely separate screens and databases (OwnerNotificationScreen). Notifications are routed based on user role (Role) and each notification is linked to a deep link that takes the user to the required action immediately.

mapPerformance.problem: Problem: Map Performance with Many Locations and Offers
mapPerformance.problemText: Loading thousands of markers on the map at once causes severe lag on the phone interface and battery consumption.
mapPerformance.solutionText: Using Marker Clustering technology to group nearby locations into a single expandable icon when zooming, while fetching data based on current screen coordinates only (Bounding Box) through Spring Boot.

imageSpeed.problem: Problem: Image Loading Speed for Places and Users
imageSpeed.problemText: Loading high-resolution images for hundreds of places and reviews consumes user data and increases waiting time.
imageSpeed.solution: Solution: Optimized Cloud Storage (Firebase Storage & Caching)
imageSpeed.solutionText: Using Firebase Storage for image uploads, relying on caching libraries in Jetpack Compose like Coil to ensure images are loaded only once and saved locally, with image compression before uploading from the backend.

futureWorkPage.title: Conclusion
futureWorkPage.subtitle: Next steps in developing JOMAP and expanding its services to better serve the community and boost tourism.

g1.title: AI Recommendation Engine
g1.desc: Build a smart system using generative AI models to automatically design personalized itineraries for users.

g2.title: Regional Expansion
g2.desc: Expand the service model to include major Arab cities and regional tourist destinations.

g3.title: Booking & Payment Integration
g3.desc: Integrate electronic payment gateways to book event tickets, restaurant tables, and activities directly within the app.

g4.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Tours
g4.desc: Provide visual and interactive guided tours inside Jordanian archeological sites like Petra and Jerash.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.text: The JOMAP project represents an integrated solution that bridges ready-made technologies with the needs of the Jordanian community, achieving a unique balance between location discovery, supporting local businesses, and activating youth volunteering to be a true digital companion in every governorate.

teamPage.title: Meet the JoMap Team
teamPage.subtitle: A creative team combining development, design, testing, and documentation to build a smarter way to discover Jordan.
teamPage.supervisorDesc: Guiding the team through planning, development, documentation, and preparing the final presentation.

musaab.name: Musaab Makahleh

abd.name: Abdulqader Frokh
abd.description: Supports the development lifecycle through front-end and back-end implementation, API integration, rigorous code testing, and optimizing overall application performance.

ziad.name: Ziad Qufeishah
ziad.description: Works on frontend implementation, page layout, animations, and making the app interface more creative and professional.

techPage.title: Technologies Used
techPage.frontendDesc: React JS is used to build the user interface with fast navigation, reusable components, and a modern interactive design.
techPage.backendDesc: Spring Boot provides REST APIs, authentication, business logic, database connection, and email sending service.
techPage.databaseDesc: The system stores user data, places, ratings, favorites, reports, and owner location data.
techPage.FullStackDesc: The team worked on both frontend and backend development, ensuring seamless
